mnemonics.title: JK Flip-Flop Truth
mnemonics.content: J-K Flip-Flop: Remember 'J sets, K kills' - J sets (Q=1), K kills/resets (Q=0). J=K=1 means TOGGLE (Q flips). J=K=0 means HOLD (Q stays).

mnemonics.title: D Flip-Flop Purpose
mnemonics.content: D = Data or Delay. Q+ = D. Simply passes input to output after clock edge - 'D' copies to Q.'

mnemonics.title: T Flip-Flop Action
mnemonics.content: T = Toggle. T=1 flips state (Q→Q̅), T=0 maintains state (Q→Q). Like a light switch - flip or stay.

mnemonics.title: Mealy vs Moore Output
mnemonics.content: Mealy: 'Mealy feeds input to output' - Output depends on input AND state. Moore: 'Moore more delayed' - Output depends only on state (extra clock cycle).

mnemonics.title: Setup/Hold Sequence
mnemonics.content: Setup before Hold: 'Students Prepare Homework' - Setup (S) comes Before Hold (H) chronologically.

acronyms.usage: Basic memory element with set and reset inputs; S=1 sets Q=1, R=1 resets Q=0, both 0 holds state.
Enhanced SR flip-flop where J=K=1 toggles output, eliminating forbidden state.
Simplest flip-flop; output equals input after clock edge. Used in registers and shift registers.
Flip-flop that toggles output when T=1; used in counters and frequency dividers.
Mathematical model describing sequential logic behavior; implemented as Mealy or Moore machines.
HDL abstraction level describing sequential operations between registers.

visualTips.visualization: Draw memory element as a box with feedback arrow returning to input. Combinational = straight-through path (no feedback). Sequential = path with loop back showing state retention.
Draw clock as square wave with rising edge marked. Show data input stable window (setup + hold) centered on edge. Output changes only at edge, not during level.
Draw states as bubbles, transitions as arrows. For Mealy: label arrows 'input/output'. For Moore: label inside bubble 'state/output', arrows 'input only'.
Draw multiple clock waveforms staggered horizontally. Show each FF output toggling when previous goes 1→0. Cumulative delay visible as offset between edges.

analogies.analogy: Sequential circuit is like a person making decisions based on both current situation AND past experiences. Combinational circuit is like a reflex - only responds to immediate input.
Flip-flop is like a light switch that STAYS in whatever position you leave it - it has memory. Press once (clock edge), it remembers your press.
Clock is like the heartbeat or rhythm of a drum in an orchestra - all musicians (flip-flops) synchronize to this beat. Without it, chaos (race conditions) ensues.
Setup time is like arriving at station before train comes. Hold time is like staying on platform after train leaves. Miss either and you miss the train (data not captured).
